title: Compound prepositions
description: Identify and use less common prepositions with multiple syllables.
topic_path: Parts of speech: the preposition and the conjunction

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the preposition that makes the most sense in this sentence.**

>The bunny hopped \_\_\_\_\_\_ the forest path.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Compound prepositions are longer and usually not as frequently used in Standard American English as common prepositions are. 

Some examples include _about, under, outside, inside, without, before, after, onto, into, beside, upon, beyond, against, among, below, until_.
We're looking for a preposition that describes the position and direction of the bunny, so we should use **"along"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
